summary: .gitignore changes are complete and correct. All 4 required patterns are present. The patterns use proper glob syntax (products/*/) that matches all current and future product directories. No over-broad patterns that could accidentally ignore needed files.

gitignore_analysis.new_section: # Internal product files (not for public repo)

patterns.purpose: Exclude product configs with absolute paths, internal ports, env var names
patterns.notes: Only matches product.yaml in direct product subdirectories, not deeper nesting

patterns.purpose: Exclude developer-facing product documentation with platform details
patterns.notes: Does not affect root CLAUDE.md or botplatform/CLAUDE.md

patterns.purpose: Exclude project-knowledge directories with server IPs and deployment details
patterns.notes: Trailing slash correctly limits to directories only

patterns.purpose: Explicit path for bananzabot credentials file
patterns.notes: More specific than the existing root .env pattern; ensures bananzabot/.env is covered even if .env pattern behavior changes

gitignore_analysis.existing_env_pattern: .env at line 6 already provides root-level .env coverage, but bananzabot/.env was tracked before the rule existed, so explicit pattern + git rm --cached was required

pre_commit_hook_fix.change: Test 1 now uses git diff --cached --name-status instead of --name-only, and filters out D (deleted) status entries
pre_commit_hook_fix.correctness: Correct. Deleting a secret from git tracking is the desired action and should not trigger a security block
pre_commit_hook_fix.edge_cases_considered: Rename (R) status not filtered — if a .env file is renamed, it still triggers. This is correct behavior.
